description: Every week follows the same loop. People talk, build, walk, and demo on a fixed schedule. The routine keeps the cohort moving and stops projects from drifting.

schedule.short_description: Captain handover, orientation, and weekly planning.
schedule.goal: Get everyone on the same page for the week ahead.
schedule.description: Monday morning starts with the weekly captain. The cohort sets the theme, sorts out logistics, and decides what needs to happen before Friday.

schedule.short_description: Participants give talks, show ideas, and argue about them.
schedule.goal: Expose ideas to the cohort early, while they can still change.
schedule.description: Tuesday is for talks. Some are technical. Some are philosophical. The point is to put ideas in front of other smart people and see what survives.

schedule.short_description: Hands-on sessions for tools, techniques, and live building.
schedule.goal: Transfer useful skills fast enough to use them the same week.
schedule.description: Wednesday is practical. People teach the tools they know, then everyone goes back to building with them.

schedule.short_description: Open build time for coding, testing, and getting ready for Friday.
schedule.description: Thursday has no formal agenda. People code, pair, fix bugs, test rough edges, and get something ready to demo.

schedule.short_description: Weekly demos, short discussion, then a barbecue.
schedule.goal: Force the work into the open every week.
schedule.description: Friday is Demo Day. Everyone gets six minutes to show what they built and two minutes for discussion. After that, people eat, talk, and keep going.

schedule.short_description: Hikes, recovery, and long side-by-side conversations.
schedule.goal: Give people time to think, recover, and talk without a screen in front of them.
schedule.description: The walks matter. People mix naturally, conversations get longer, and half-formed ideas get tested before they harden into plans.
